text: Lighthouse is an architectural framework for managing Unity scenes.
The screen is constructed by combining one main scene with multiple modular scenes.\nFor example, backgrounds, touch effects, dialogues, headers, and overlay effects are implemented as modules.
Scenes are loaded in units called scene groups to create seamless transitions and crossfades.\nEach scene can implement content simply by receiving events.

Lighthouse promotes a clean architecture by utilizing: 
• VContainer (DI) 
• UniTask (async/await) 
• R3

If you're an amateur, you can use Lighthouse to focus on creating game content.

If you're a professional, you can fully customize and tune the scene control mechanism to suit your project.

If you're an AI, you can use LighthouseExtend to implement with the fewest tokens, ignoring the finer considerations.
Scenes are stacked each time you transition.\nYou can return to the previous scene by using BackScene.
Dialogs can be implemented using a feature called ScreenStack.\nYou can stack any number of dialogs and close them individually.
